description:
Goal: finish the source-production mini-epic by deleting obsolete compiler2 assumptions, proving the fz2 surface, and documenting the new boundary.

Signal:
- compiler2 no longer emits or depends on errors like `expected expanded AST without item macro calls` or `expected expanded macro-free call` for supported macro/sugar surface
- fz2 fixture coverage includes macro, cross-module macro, item macro, pipe/headless case, capture/lambda sugar, and a negative require/import diagnostic
- telemetry tests assert source-production facts/waits rather than poking world internals
- docs are updated: `northstar.html`, `.agent/docs/pipeline.md`, `.agent/docs/modules.md`, `.agent/docs/parser-syntax.md`, `.agent/docs/telemetry.md`, and any user guide that still describes the old frontend desugar/macro pass as the compiler2 path
- any old helper that remains for the retiring legacy compiler is named/documented as legacy-owned and not imported by compiler2

Change:
- remove compiler2 use of old frontend macro/desugar machinery and partial AST-value codecs on the fz2 path
- delete or rewrite stale compiler2 source-boundary tests/docs that assume pre-expanded input
- add source-production telemetry/event docs and fixture harness assertions
- update `fz-rh2.11.5` if needed so it becomes a short closeout ticket rather than re-specifying this mini-epic

Why:
- the construction affordances must not remain as permanent alternate paths
- finishing here returns the arc to the original surface-parity checkpoint with a smaller, truthful problem
title: Compiler2 source-production closeout: remove old frontend assumptions and update docs
